info.name: Maisa Workflows API
info.description:
API calls used by the Maisa GitHub Actions workflows.

AUTH: Cookie-based. Set the 'session_id' variable to the value of your 'maisaai-session-id' cookie (get it from DevTools → Application → Cookies after logging in to the Maisa UI).

BASE URL: The real base URL includes a path prefix, e.g. https://gppaas-maisa.dev.sgtech.corp/maisa-bff. Set 'base_url' to the full base including /maisa-bff.

NOTE: All responses are wrapped in {"data": ...}.

variable.description: Full API base URL including path prefix (e.g. /maisa-bff). Change per environment.
Value of the 'maisaai-session-id' cookie. Get it from DevTools → Application → Cookies after logging in to the Maisa UI. This is what goes in MAISA_API_KEYS_JSON for the CI/CD workflows.
Organization ID. Use 'Discover org ID' request to find it.
Workspace ID. Use 'List workspaces' request to find it.
Worker Manager ID (the parent entity). Use 'List worker managers' to find it.
Worker version ID (lastVersionId from the worker manager). Used for export.

item.name: 1. Setup — Discover IDs
item.description: Run these first to populate org_id and workspace_id variables.

request.description:
Returns a list of organizations. Copy the 'id' (or '_id') from the first item into the 'org_id' collection variable.

Response shape: { "data": [ { "id": "...", "name": "..." } ] }

Returns all workspaces in the organization. Copy the target workspace 'id' into the 'workspace_id' collection variable.

Response shape: { "data": [ { "id": "...", "name": "..." } ] }

Used in export-workers.yml to list all worker managers in a workspace. Pagination: check response.pagination.hasNextPage and increment page.

From each worker manager, grab:
  - id → wm_id
  - name → used for the filename slug
  - lastVersionId → worker_version_id (used for export)
  - status, totalRuns (metadata)

Response shape: { "data": [ { "id": "...", "name": "...", "lastVersionId": "...", "deployedVersionId": "...", "status": "..." } ], "pagination": { "hasNextPage": false } }

Exports a specific worker version. Use 'lastVersionId' from the worker manager as :versionId.

The response bytes are saved as a .b64 file in Git:
  workers/<workspace_name>/<timestamp>/<slug>__worker__<wm_id>.b64

If the bytes are valid UTF-8 they are saved as-is; otherwise they are base64-encoded first.

Corresponds to: GET /workers/{workerId}/export (operationId: ExportWorker)

Imports a worker into a specific workspace. The 'definition' field contains the content read from the .b64 snapshot file in Git.

Workflow inputs that map here:
  - target_organization_id → :orgId
  - target_workspace_id    → :wsId

Corresponds to: POST /organizations/{organizationId}/workspaces/{workspaceId}/workers/import (operationId: ImportWorker)
